meta.note: Manually maintained. The EAA is a fixed legislative text (adopted 2019-04-17, applicable 2025-06-28). No automated update needed.

directive.title: Directive (EU) 2019/882 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 17 April 2019 on the accessibility requirements for products and services

articles.title: Subject matter
articles.summary: Establishes common accessibility requirements for certain products and services to improve the functioning of the internal market.

articles.title: Scope
articles.summary: Defines the products and services covered, including e-commerce services, banking services, electronic communications, and access to audiovisual media services.

articles.title: Accessibility requirements
articles.summary: Requires that products and services comply with the accessibility requirements set out in Annex I. For services, Section III of Annex I applies.

articles.title: Obligations of service providers
articles.summary: Service providers shall ensure that they design and provide services in accordance with the accessibility requirements of this Directive (Annex I, Section III).

articles.title: Disproportionate burden
articles.summary: Economic operators may claim exemption from accessibility requirements if compliance would impose a disproportionate burden, assessed per Annex VI criteria.

articles.title: Presumption of conformity
articles.summary: Products and services conforming to harmonised standards (EN 301 549) or parts thereof shall be presumed to be in conformity with the accessibility requirements covered by those standards.

annexI.title: Annex I — Accessibility requirements for products and services

sections.title: Services
sections.summary: Accessibility requirements applicable to all services covered by Article 2. This is the primary section relevant to web accessibility.

requirements.title: Information provision
requirements.description: Service providers shall provide information about the functioning of the service and its accessibility features in accordance with Section I requirements, making it available via more than one sensory channel.

requirements.title: Websites accessibility
requirements.description: Websites, including related online applications, shall be made accessible in a consistent and adequate way for users' perception, operation and understanding, including the adaptability of content presentation and interaction, and when necessary providing an accessible electronic alternative.

requirements.title: Mobile applications accessibility
requirements.description: Mobile-based services, including mobile applications, shall be made accessible in a consistent and adequate way for users' perception, operation and understanding.

requirements.title: Electronic identification and security
requirements.description: Electronic identification, security and payment methods shall be made perceivable, operable, understandable and robust.

requirements.title: Support services
requirements.description: Where support services (help desks, call centres, technical support, relay services) are provided, they shall provide information on the accessibility of the service and its compatibility with assistive technologies, in accessible modes of communication.

sections.title: Specific requirements for services
sections.summary: Additional requirements for specific service categories (electronic communications, audiovisual media, transport, banking, e-commerce, e-books).

serviceCategories.title: Electronic communications services
serviceCategories.description: Real-time text, total conversation services where video is provided, emergency communications support.

serviceCategories.title: Audiovisual media services
serviceCategories.description: Electronic programme guides shall be perceivable, operable, understandable and robust. Audiovisual content shall be accessible.

serviceCategories.title: Air, bus, rail and waterborne passenger transport services
serviceCategories.description: Websites, mobile applications, electronic tickets, real-time travel information and interactive self-service terminals shall be accessible.

serviceCategories.title: Consumer banking services
serviceCategories.description: Identification methods, electronic signatures, security and payment services shall be perceivable, operable, understandable and robust.

serviceCategories.title: E-books and dedicated software
serviceCategories.description: E-books shall provide text-to-speech capability, navigation, dynamic layout, alternative descriptions, and DRM that does not block accessibility features.

serviceCategories.title: E-commerce services
serviceCategories.description: Identification, security and payment methods used in e-commerce shall be accessible.

wcagCoverage.description: The EAA effectively requires WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance for web services via EN 301 549. The following maps each WCAG 2.1 A/AA success criterion to the relevant EAA provision.

annexVI.title: Annex VI — Criteria for assessment of disproportionate burden
annexVI.summary: Criteria for assessing whether accessibility requirements impose a disproportionate burden: ratio of net costs of compliance to overall costs, estimated costs and benefits for the economic operator, and ratio of net costs to net turnover.
criteria: Ratio of the net costs of compliance with the accessibility requirements to the overall costs (operating and capital expenditures) of manufacturing, distributing or importing the product or providing the service to the economic operator

Estimated costs and benefits to the economic operators, including in relation to production processes and investments, in relation to the estimated benefit for persons with disabilities, taking into account the amount and frequency of use of the specific product or service
Ratio of the net costs of compliance with the accessibility requirements to the net turnover of the economic operator
